title: Configuration Drift Between Staging and Production Environments
symptoms: Feature working correctly in staging but failing in production
Database connection pool exhaustion in production not reproducible in staging
Ansible playbook showing 47 configuration differences between environments
Manual hotfix records found in production change log but not in Ansible code
Environment comparison tool showing critical parameter mismatches
root_cause: Accumulated configuration drift from 6 months of manual production hotfixes that were never backported to the Ansible configuration code or applied to staging
resolution: Audited all 47 configuration differences, reconciled configurations by updating Ansible playbooks with production values, reapplied configuration to both environments, and verified parity
preventive_actions: Implement configuration drift detection running daily with alerting
Enforce infrastructure-as-code policy prohibiting manual production changes
Create automated environment parity verification in CI pipeline
Schedule monthly configuration audit between staging and production
Implement change approval process requiring Ansible code updates for all changes

timeline.event: Feature deployment fails in production despite passing staging tests
Initial investigation unable to reproduce in staging
Critical drift in database connection pool settings identified
Full audit of all configuration differences initiated
Ansible playbooks updated with reconciled configurations
Configurations applied to both environments, parity verified

embedding_summary: Configuration drift of 47 differences discovered between staging and production environments accumulated from 6 months of manual production hotfixes not backported to Ansible code. Feature deployment failures resulted. Resolved by auditing and reconciling all configurations in Ansible playbooks.
